name: Leader

src.description: Before the battle, CHARACTER units with the Leader ability can be attached to one of their Bodyguard units to form an Attached unit.

note: Generated oversimplifies: actual mechanic is pre-battle attachment to Bodyguard unit only, not generic leadership.

name: Waaagh! Energy

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, add 1 to the Strength and Damage characteristics of this model’s ’Eadbanger weapon for every 5 models in that unit (rounding down), but while that unit contains 10 or more models, that weapon has the [HAZARDOUS] ability.

note: Generated lists flat +1 S/D; actual ability scales weapon damage per 5-model threshold and adds Hazardous at 10+.

name: Da Jump (Psychic)

src.description: Once per turn, at the end of your Movement phase, one WEIRDBOY from your army can use this ability. If it does, roll one D6:on a 1, that WEIRDBOY’s unit suffers D6 mortal wounds; on a 2+, remove that WEIRDBOY’s unit from the battlefield and set it up again anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9"horizontally away from all enemy models.

note: Missing success condition (2+) and displacement mechanic; only states failure mortal wound outcome.

name: Drill Through

src.description: Each time this model ends a Charge move, select one enemy unit within Engagement Range of it and roll one D6:on a 2‑5, that enemy unit suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 6, that enemy unit suffers 3 mortal wounds.

note: Trigger condition vague (end charge move). Missing roll conditions: 2-5 deals D3, 6 deals 3.

name: Drive-by Dakka

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets a unit within 9",improve the Armour Penetration characteristic of that attack by 1.

note: Range and target scope unclear; actual improves AP per attack within 9", not conditional on target attribute.

name: Deff from Above

src.description: Each time this unit ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved over during that move and roll one D6 for each model in this unit:for each 4+, that enemy unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Missing roll per model condition; actual rolls D6 per model moved over, each 4+ causes 1 mortal wound.

name: Rivetin’ Dakka

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model has shot, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks made with a rivet kannon. Until the start of your next turn, that enemy unit is suppressed. While a unit is suppressed, each time a model in that unit makes a ranged attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.

note: Omits trigger (after shooting) and weapon condition (rivet kannon); suppressed state applies to hit rolls until next turn.

name: Hold Still and Say ‘Aargh!’

src.description: Each time an attack made by this model with its ’urty syringe scores a Critical Wound against a unit (excluding VEHICLE units), that unit suffers D6 mortal wounds.

note: Missing weapon and attack type conditions; only triggers on Critical Wound with specific weapon.

name: Shooty Power Trip

src.description:
Each time this unit is selected to shoot, you can roll one D6:

■ On a 1‑2, this unit suffers D3 mortal wounds.
■ On a 3‑4, until the end of the phase, add 1 to the Strength characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by models in this unit.
■ On a 5‑6, until the end of the phase, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of ranged weapons equipped by models in this unit.

note: Lists all outcomes simultaneously; actual is roll D6 with mutually exclusive outcomes: D3 wounds, +1 S, or +1 A.

name: Dat’s Our Loot!

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack, re-roll a Hit roll of 1. If that attack targets a unit that is within range of an objective marker, you can re-roll the Hit roll instead.

note: Condition unclear; actual grants auto reroll 1s (always), and conditional reroll any hit vs objective-near targets.

name: Shokk-boosta

src.description: You can re‐roll Advance rolls made for this model’s unit. In addition, each time this model’s unit makes a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move, models in that unit can move through models and terrain features. When doing so, they can move within Engagement Range of such models but cannot end that move within Engagement Range of them, and any Desperate Escape test is automatically passed.

note: Syntax jumbled; missing second-order conditions (cannot end within ER of models, DE test auto-pass).

name: Dead Brutal

src.description: While the Waaagh! is active for your army, this model’s ’uge choppa has a Damage characteristic of 3.

note: Missing Waaagh! active condition; applies only when Waaagh! active for army.

name: Blastajet Attack Run

src.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack that targets a unit that cannot FLY, re-roll a Hit roll of 1.

note: Target condition wrong; actual specifies non-FLY targets only.

name: Blastajet Force Field

src.description: The bearer has a 4+ invulnerable save, but it loses the GRENADES keyword.

note: Missing side effect: bearer loses GRENADES keyword.

name: ’Ard Case

src.description: Add 2 to the bearer’s Toughness characteristic, but it no longer has the Firing Deck ability.

note: Missing downside: bearer loses Firing Deck ability.

name: Kunnin’ Infiltrator

src.description: Once per battle, in your Movement phase, instead of making a Normal move with this model’s unit, you can remove it from the battlefield and set it up again anywhere on the battlefield that is more than 9"horizontally away from all enemy models.

note: Generated too vague; actual is specific tactic: once per battle, replace Normal move with redeploy.

name: Big an’ Shooty

src.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack, if the Waaagh! is active for your army, add 1 to the Hit roll.

note: Missing Waaagh! active condition requirement.

name: Prophet of Da Great Waaagh!

src.description: While this unit is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit makes a melee attack, add 1 to the Hit roll and add 1 to the Wound roll and if the Waaagh! is active for your army, a Critical Hit is scored on a successful unmodified Hit roll of 5+.

note: Omits Waaagh! active condition for critical hit on 5+ bonus.

name: Ghazghkull’s Waaagh! Banner (Aura)

src.description: While a friendly ORKS unit is within 12"of Makari, if the Waaagh! is active for your army, melee weapons equipped by models in that unit have the [LETHAL HITS] ability.

note: Missing Waaagh! active condition; only applies when Waaagh! active for army.

name: Attached Unit

src.description: If a CHARACTER unit from your army with the Leader ability can be attached to a BOYZ unit, it can be attached to this unit instead.

note: Generated vague; actual describes conditional attachment eligibility for Leaders to specific unit type.

name: Tank Hunters

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets a MONSTER or VEHICLE unit, add 1 to the Hit roll and add 1 to the Wound roll.

note: Missing targeting condition: only vs MONSTER or VEHICLE units, not all targets.

name: Pulsa Rokkit

src.description: Once per battle, when the bearer’s unit is selected to shoot in your Shooting phase, the bearer can use its pulsa rokkit. If it does, until the end of the phase, improve the Strength and Armour Penetration characteristics of ranged weapons equipped by models in the bearer’s unit by 1.

note: Missing Strength boost, incomplete mechanic description, not just AP.

name: Sneaky Gitz

src.description: Enemy units cannot use the Fire Overwatch Stratagem to shoot at this unit.

note: Inverted: prevents enemy Fire Overwatch, not unit gains ability.

name: Distraction Grot

src.description: Once per battle, in your opponent’s Shooting phase, before making a saving throw for a model in this unit, it can deploy the distraction grot. If it does, until the end of the phase, models in this unit have a 5+ invulnerable save.

note: Specifies 5+ save; missing once-per-battle and opponent's phase trigger.

name: Patrol Squad

src.description:
At the start of the Declare Battle Formations step this unit can be split into two units, each containing five models.
(when splitting a unit in this way, make a note of which models form each of the two new units. If you are splitting a unit that is equipped with 1 bomb squig and/or 1 distraction grot, only one of the new units can use that ability during the battle – make a note of which of the new units this will be).

note: Combat Squads vague; actual split is into specific 5-model units.

name: Get Da Good Bitz

src.description: At the end of your Command phase, if this unit is within range of an objective marker you control, that objective marker remains under your control until your opponent’s Level of Control over that objective marker is greater than yours at the end of a phase.

note: Completely wrong: affects objective control, not OC modifier.

name: Bodyguard

src.description: If this unit has a Starting Strength of 20, you can attach up to two Leader units to it instead of one (but only if one of those is a WARBOSS unit). If you do, and this unit is destroyed, the Leader units attached to it become separate units with their original Starting Strengths.

note: Wrong mechanics: controls leader attachment slots and posthumous separation.

name: Grot Riggers

src.description: At the start of your Command phase, this model regains 1 lost wound.

note: Right concept but scope/frequency unclear; only regains 1 wound at Command phase.

name: Sawbonez

src.description: At the end of your Movement phase, select one friendly BEAST SNAGGA CHARACTER model within 3"of this model. That model is healed and regains up to 3 lost wounds. Each model can only be healed once per turn.

note: Targets single CHARACTER, not heal aura; healing limited once per turn.

name: Big an’ Stompy

src.description: Each time this model makes a melee attack, if the Waaagh! is active for your army, add 1 to the Hit roll.

note: Missing Waaagh! dependency, applies to melee not all attacks.

name: Splat!

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack that targets a unit that is at its Starting Strength (excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES), re‑roll a Hit roll of 1.

note: Unclear 'A'; misses Starting Strength check and vehicle/monster exclusion.

name: Gun-crazy Show-offs

src.description: Each time a model in this unit targets the closest eligible target with its snazzgun, until the end of the phase, that weapon has an Attacks characteristic of 4.

note: Garbled condition; applies to closest eligible target with snazzgun.

name: Ammo Runt

src.description: Once per battle, when this unit is selected to shoot, it can use this ability. If it does, until the end of the phase, ranged weapons equipped by models in this unit have the [LETHAL HITS] ability.

note: Missing once-per-battle constraint; all ranged weapons, not just unit.

name: Boom Bomb

src.description: Each time this model ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved over during that move and roll one D6:on a 4+, that unit suffers D6 mortal wounds.

note: Triggers on Normal move only, needs 4+ roll, applies per unit moved over.

name: Piston-driven Brutality

src.description: Each time this model ends a Charge move, select one enemy unit within Engagement Range of it and roll one D6:on a 2-5, that enemy unit suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 6, that enemy unit suffers D3+3 mortal wounds.

note: Wrong damage: D3 on 2-5, D6 on 6, triggers on Charge move within ER.

name: Unstable Oracle

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, add 2 to the Attacks characteristic of this model’s Eyez of Mork weapon for every 5 models in that unit (rounding down), but while that unit contains 10 or more models, that weapon has the [HAZARDOUS] ability.

note: Wrong mechanic: scales Eyez of Mork A by unit size, gains HAZARDOUS.

name: Roar of Mork (Psychic)

src.description: In your opponent’s Command phase, you can select one enemy unit within 18"of and visible to this PSYKER and roll one D6:on a 1, this PSYKER’s unit suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 2+, until the start of your opponent’s next Command phase, that enemy unit is confrazzled. While a unit is confrazzled, each time a Battle‑shock or Leadership test is taken for it, subtract 2 from that test.

note: Oversimplified: confrazzled is debuff on 2+; 1 causes self-wound.

name: Dakkastorm

src.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack, every successful Hit roll scores a Critical Hit.

note: Completely wrong: every Hit is Critical, not -1 to hit rolls.

name: Fix Dat Armour Up

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, in your Command phase, you can return 1 destroyed Bodyguard model to that unit.

note: Missing scope: only returns Bodyguard models, only in Command phase.

name: Kustom Force Field

src.description: While the bearer is leading a unit, models in that unit have a 4+ invulnerable save against ranged attacks.

note: Specifies save value; missing ranged-attack-only restriction.

name: Grot Oiler

src.description: Once per battle, at the end of your Movement phase, one model in the bearer’s unit regains D3 lost wounds.

note: Single model heals D3; once-per-battle at Movement phase end.

name: Buzzer Squigs

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model has shot, select one enemy unit (excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES) hit by one or more of those attacks made with squig‑launchas and roll one D6:on a 4+, until the end of your opponent’s next turn, that enemy unit is hindered. While a unit is hindered, subtract 2"from its Move characteristic and subtract 2 from Advance and Charge rolls made for it.

note: Hindered debuff correct but unclear trigger; only non-MONSTER/-VEHICLE.

name: Squig Mine

src.description:
Once per battle, at the start of any phase, select one enemy unit within 3"of this model and roll one D6:on a 4+, that enemy unit suffers D6 mortal wounds.

Designer’s Note:Place a Squig Mine token next to the model, removing it once this ability has been used.

note: Range is 3", not 6"; once-per-battle at any phase start.

name: Monster Hunters

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes an attack that targets a MONSTER or VEHICLE unit, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

note: Missing targeting restriction: only vs MONSTER or VEHICLE.

name: Fuel-mixa Grot

src.description: Each time this model’s unit Advances, do not make an Advance roll for it. Instead, until the end of the phase, add 6"to the Move characteristic of models in that unit.

note: Bonus applies when Advancing; replaces Advance roll with flat boost.

name: Know-wotz

src.description: While this model is within 3"of one or more friendly ORKS VEHICLE units, this model has the Lone Operative ability.

note: Gain Lone Operative near VEHICLE units; range is 3", not 6".

name: Mekaniak

src.description: At the end of your Movement phase, you can select one friendly ORKS VEHICLE model within 3"of this model. That VEHICLE model regains up to D3 lost wounds, and, until the start of your next Movement phase, each time that VEHICLE model makes an attack, add 1 to the Hit roll. Each model can only be selected for this ability once per turn.

note: Wrong: heals and boosts Hit once per turn per VEHICLE within 3".

name: One Last Kill

src.description: While this model is leading a unit, each time a model in that unit is destroyed by a melee attack, if it has not fought this phase, roll one D6:on a 4+, do not remove it from play. The destroyed model can fight after the attacking unit has finished making its attacks, and is then removed from play.

note: Fights-on-death applies per unit member; 4+ roll required.

name: Da Bigger Dey Iz…

src.description: Each time this model makes a melee attack that targets a MONSTER or VEHICLE unit, add 1 to the Damage characteristic of that attack. Each time this model makes a melee attack that targets a TITANIC unit, add 2 to the Damage characteristic of that attack instead.

note: TITANIC adds +2 D; +1 D for other MONSTER/VEHICLE only.

name: Super Runts

src.description:
While this model is leading a unit:

■ Models in that unit have the Scouts 9"ability.
■ Each time a model in that unit makes an attack, add 1 to the Hit roll and add 1 to the Wound roll.
■ Each time an attack targets that unit, subtract 1 from the Wound roll.

note: Missing Scouts 9" and wound roll subtraction; incomplete ability list.

name: Special Dose

src.description: While the Waaagh! is active for your army, add 6"to the Move characteristic of models in this model’s unit.

note: Requires Waaagh! active; permanent not conditional.

name: Runtherd

src.description: Each time an attack targets this unit, if it contains one or more Gretchin models, until that attack is resolved, Runtherd models in this unit have a Toughness characteristic of 2.

note: Only when Gretchin present; conditional Toughness in unit.

name: Thievin’ Scavengers

src.description: At the start of your Movement phase, roll one D6 for each objective marker you control that has one or more units from your army with this ability within range of it (excluding Battle-shocked units). If one or more of those rolls is a 4+, you gain 1CP.

note: Rolls per objective controlled, need 4+; excludes Battle-shocked.

name: Deranged Snotling Assault

src.description: In your Shooting phase, after this model has shot, select one enemy unit hit by one or more of those attacks; that unit must take a Battle-shock test.

note: After model shoots, not generic phase effect.

name: Grot Assistant

src.description: Once per battle, after rolling to determine how many attacks the bearer’s shokk attack gun makes, you can re-roll that dice.

note: Specific to shokk attack gun attack roll reroll.

name: Spirit of Gork (Psychic)

src.description: At the start of the Fight phase, you can select one friendly ORKS unit within 12"of this model and roll one D6:on a 1, this model suffers D3 mortal wounds; on a 2‑5, until the end of the phase, add 1 to the Strength characteristic of melee weapons equipped by models in that unit; on a 6, until the end of the phase, add 1 to the Strength characteristic of melee weapons equipped by models in that unit and those weapons have the [LETHAL HITS] ability.

note: Outcome depends on D6 roll; 1 backfires with mortal wounds.

name: Pyromaniaks

src.description: Each time a model in this unit makes a ranged attack with a burna that targets an enemy unit within 6",re‑roll a Wound roll of 1. If the target of that attack is also within range of an objective marker, you can re‑roll the Wound roll instead.

note: Only with burna weapons; can reroll if target on objective.

name: Da Boss’ Ladz

src.description: While a WARBOSS model is leading this unit, each time an attack targets this unit, if the Strength characteristic of that attack is greater than the Toughness characteristic of this unit, subtract 1 from the Wound roll.

note: Requires WARBOSS leader; only when Strength > Toughness.

name: Single-minded Predator

src.description: You can target this model’s unit with the Heroic Intervention Stratagem for 0CP, and can do so even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase.

note: Heroic Intervention for 0 CP; unclear what '?' in refund means.

name: Krumpin’ Time

src.description: While the Waaagh! is active for your army, models in this unit have the Feel No Pain 5+ ability.

note: Requires Waaagh! active; otherwise correct.

name: Billowing Fumes (Aura)

src.description: While an enemy unit (excluding MONSTERS and VEHICLES) is within 6"of this model, each time a model in that unit makes an attack, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.

note: Excludes MONSTER/VEHICLE enemies; affects their attacks.

name: Gargantuan

src.description: Each time this model makes a Normal, Advance or Fall Back move, it can move over models (excluding TITANIC models) and terrain features that are 4"or less in height as if they were not there.

note: Terrain height limit 4"; excludes TITANIC models.

name: Walking Bastion

src.description: This model does not suffer the penalty to its Hit rolls for making ranged attacks while enemy units are within Engagement Range of it.

note: Engagement Range penalty only; applies to ranged attacks.

name: Burna Bomb

src.description: Each time this model ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved over during that move. Until the end of the turn, models in that unit cannot have the Benefit of Cover. In addition, roll one D6 for each model in that unit:for each 6, that unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Denies cover; roll D6 per model for mortal wound on 6.

name: On Da Hunt

src.description: For each model embarked within this TRANSPORT, add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of this model’s butcha boyz weapon (to a maximum of +6).

note: Scales by embarked models to max +6; specific butcha boyz weapon.

name: Da Biggest and da Best

src.description: While the Waaagh! is active for your army, add 4 to the Attacks characteristic of this model’s melee weapons.

note: Requires Waaagh! active; melee weapons only.

name: Aerial Deployment

src.description: If this model starts the game in Strategic Reserves, it can be set up in the Reinforcements step of your first, second or third Movement phase, regardless of any mission rules.

note: Garbled text; allows reinforcement in turns 1-3 from reserves.

name: Big Bomms

src.description: Once per battle, after the bearer ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved across during that move and roll six D6: for each 4+, that unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Once-per-battle; rolls six D6, not implicit count.

name: Interceptor

src.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack that targets a unit that can Fly, add 1 to the Hit roll.

note: Missing target restriction: only vs FLYING units.

name: Small Bomms

src.description: Each time the bearer ends a Normal move, you can select one enemy unit it moved across during that move and roll three D6: for each 4+, that unit suffers 1 mortal wound.

note: Rolls three D6 per crossed unit; permanent, not once-per-battle.

name: Strafing Run

src.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack that targets a unit that cannot Fly, add 1 to the Hit roll.

note: Wrong target: +1 Hit vs non-FLYING, not FLYING units.

name: Big Booms

src.description: In your Shooting phase, just after selecting a target for this model’s supa-kannon, roll one D6 for the target unit and every other unit within 3" of that unit: on a 5+, the unit being rolled for is struck by a concussive wave. After this model has finished making its attacks against that target unit this phase, each unit struck by a concussive wave this phase suffers D3 mortal wounds.

note: Scope wrong (aura 6 vs 3); misses concussive wave roll (5+) targeting timing

name: Da Bigger Dey Are, da Better Dey Drop

src.description: Each time an attack made with this model’s lifta-droppa destroys an enemy model that has the Deadly Demise ability, that model’s Deadly Demise ability inflicts mortal wounds on a D6 roll of 3+ instead of on a 6.

note: Completely wrong mechanic; triggers on Deadly Demise not lifta-droppa attack

name: Da Biggest Booms

src.description: In your Shooting phase, just after selecting a target for this model’s krusha kannon, the target unit and every other enemy Infantry unit within 3" of that unit must take a Battle-shock test.

note: Scope wrong (aura 6 vs 3); missing Infantry unit restriction

name: Rolling Fortress

src.description: Each time a ranged attack is allocated to an Orks model from your army, if that model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this Deff Rolla Battle Fortress model, that model has the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

note: Scope/mechanics wrong; applies to self model visibility not unit benefit

name: Has Yoo Been a Good Little Grot This Year?

src.description: Once per battle, in your Shooting phase, you can target this model’s unit with the Grenade Stratagem for 0CP, and can do so even if you have already targeted a different unit with that Stratagem this phase.

note: Completely misses stratagem-specific interaction; refund is misleading

name: BIG GUNZ

src.description: Designer’s Note: Place two Grot Crew tokens next to each Mek Gun model when this unit is first set up, removing one each time its Big Gun model loses a wound (a Big Gun model itself is considered to represent its final wound).

note: Flavor text label unhelpful; real mechanic is token management per wound

name: SPEED FREEKS

src.description: If a model from your army with the Leader ability can be attached to a Warbikers unit, it can be attached to this unit instead.

note: Vague; actual mechanic is about unit eligibility for certain leaders

name: Furious Barrage

src.description: Each time this model makes a ranged attack that targets an enemy unit that contains five or more models, you can re-roll the Hit roll.

note: Misses target unit size requirement (5+ models)

name: Waaagh!
note: Missing invulnerable save (5+) and charge eligibility after Advance

name: Ramshackle Cover

src.description: Each time a ranged attack is allocated to a model, if that model is not fully visible to every model in the attacking unit because of this FORTIFICATION, that model has the Benefit of Cover against that attack.

note: Generic flavor text; actual mechanic is visibility-based cover for fortification

name: Fortification

src.description:
While an enemy unit is only within Engagement Range of one or more FORTIFICATIONS from your army:

■ That unit can still be selected as the target of ranged attacks, but each time such an attack is made, unless it is made with a Pistol, subtract 1 from the Hit roll.
■ Models in that unit do not need to take Desperate Escape tests due to Falling Back while Battle-shocked, except for those that will move over enemy models when doing so.

note: Generic flavor; actual mechanics are engagement range -1 Hit and Desperate Escape
